title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and deployment safeguards rather than new policy mandates. Fresh work focuses on how to assess jailbreak risk under limited budgets, audit model safety without benchmark labels, verify citations in research agents, and operationalize safer coding and cyber access with tighter controls.

items.title: Cited but Not Verified examines source attribution failures in deep research agents
items.why_it_matters: Citation reliability is becoming a core trust issue for agentic research systems.

items.title: When No Benchmark Exists proposes comparative LLM safety scoring without ground-truth labels
items.why_it_matters: It offers an audit framework for deployments lacking established safety benchmarks.

items.title: How Many Iterations to Jailbreak studies dynamic budget allocation for multi-turn LLM evaluation
items.why_it_matters: It could make jailbreak testing more efficient and comparable across models.

items.title: Running Codex safely details sandboxing, approvals, and telemetry for coding agents
items.why_it_matters: It shows concrete controls for reducing agent misuse and limiting execution risk.

items.title: METR reviews Anthropic’s automated R&D risk analysis
items.why_it_matters: Independent review sharpens scrutiny of frontier-model capability and risk claims.

items.title: Scaling Trusted Access for Cyber expands gated defender access to GPT-5.5 models
items.why_it_matters: Controlled access programs are becoming a key governance tool for dual-use capabilities.

items.title: GPT-5.5 Instant System Card
items.why_it_matters: System cards remain a primary disclosure mechanism for model risks and mitigations.

items.title: Introducing Trusted Contact in ChatGPT
items.why_it_matters: It extends product safety design into crisis-response workflows for self-harm concerns.

items.title: How ChatGPT learns about the world while protecting privacy
items.why_it_matters: Privacy controls and data-use limits directly affect user trust and compliance posture.

items.title: Advancing Transparency of AI-Generated Media in the EU Code of Practice
items.why_it_matters: Media provenance rules are central to platform accountability and synthetic-content governance.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
